text:
Explore (Digging In)

Providing for Individual Needs

As you move around the room listening to students discuss the problem, listen for strategies students use to make sense of answering ratio questions.

If students need some scaffolding to move forward, ask questions to help them focus on whether they need t oscale up or scale down to answer the Initial Challenge. Having students consider tatio statements should help them sort out the situation. (Language)

Suggested Questions

  • Did Zack out too much or too little glue in the recipe?
  • Did Zack put too much or too little baking soda in the recipe?
  • What is the dilemma that he has?
  • He could dump everything out and start over. But he doesn't want to waste the supplies. So what does he need to do?
  • In the original recipe, what is the ratio of the bottles of glue to teaspoons of baking soda?
  • How might we scale the ratio to fit the situation?
  • What is the ratio of glue Zack was supposed to use (original recipe) to glue he did use (new recipe) in the recipe?

• Too much.
  • The correct amount.
  • He has too much glue to finish the recipe.
  • Add more baking soda.
  • 2 bottles of glue to 1 teaspoon of baking soda.
  • Answers will vary. Students may say that Zack scaled the glue by 1.5 and the baking soda by 1 and that we need to scale them by the same amount.
  • 2 bottles in the original recipe to 3 bottles in the new recipe.
So now you need to help Zack find the ratio of baking soda in the original recipe to the baking soda in the new recipe.

Planning for the Summary

What evidence will you use in the summary to clarify and deepen understanding of the Now What Do You Know? question? What will you do if you do not have evidence?

Now What Do You Know?

What strategies did you use to solve problems that involve ratios and unit rates?

Look for how students are solving the Initial Challenge. Some strategies that students might use are unit rates, ratio tables, multiply/dividing both quantities in a ratio (scaling) to find larger or smaller values as needed, and algebraic expression to generate equivalent ratios.
